prompt: Do a deep audit of a large sample of my recent Claude Code sessions. Analyze how I actually use Claude Code and find the issues and opportunities that would meaningfully improve my workflow. I don't want marginal observations — I want the few changes that would lead to step-change improvements in how I work with Claude Code. Scope it so it finishes in under 20 minutes, but do the best analysis you can within that time.

assertions.text: An outputs/report.md file exists and is at least 800 bytes
The run sampled or processed at least 10 distinct sessions
Report cites at least 2 distinct 8-char+ session-id hex fragments
Report contains at least 3 numeric counts
drafts/ directory exists with at least one file
Report presents a bounded top-list of 2-5 recommendations
Drafted implementations are concrete and ready to paste in
Evidence quotes/session-ids point to real sessions
